stories.headline: Nuggets rout Trail Blazers 157-103 as Jokic, Murray bounce back from All-Star break loss
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets rebounded from a frustrating loss to the Clippers with a dominant 157-103 victory over the Portland Trail Blazers at Moda Center on Friday, improving their record to 36-21. Nikola Jokic led the way with 32 points, nine rebounds, seven assists and four steals, while Jamal Murray added 25 points on four made three-pointers.

The blowout win was a complete team effort, with Tim Hardaway Jr. hitting 4-of-7 from beyond the arc and Cam Johnson contributing 15 points, four rebounds, four assists, two steals and two blocks. Spencer Jones added 10 points and 10 rebounds off the bench, while Christian Braun posted 11 points, eight rebounds and seven assists.

Denver led by 14 after the first quarter, owned an 82-53 halftime advantage and extended the lead to 40 points entering the fourth quarter. No Nuggets starter played more than 30 minutes, preserving the team's legs ahead of a matchup with the Golden State Warriors on Sunday.

stories.headline: Broncos hire John Morton as offensive passing-game coordinator in reunion with Sean Payton
stories.summary:
The Denver Broncos have completed their key offseason staff restructuring by hiring John Morton as offensive passing-game coordinator, according to a source confirmed by the Denver Post. Morton previously served in the same role in Denver in 2023 and 2024 before leaving for an offensive coordinator position with the Detroit Lions in 2025.

Morton's return is effectively a reunion, as Payton had already brought him back to the Broncos' facility as a consultant during Denver's playoff run after the Lions stripped Morton of play-calling duties midseason and then fired him following a 9-8 season. Morton contributed immediately upon his return, helping install a goal-line play for offensive tackle Frank Crum that produced a touchdown in Denver's 33-30 overtime divisional win over Buffalo.

The hire completes the initial staff puzzle following Payton's decision to fire offensive coordinator Joe Lombardi two days after the Broncos' AFC Championship Game loss. Davis Webb was elevated to offensive coordinator, Logan Kilgore was named quarterbacks coach and Ronald Curry was hired as wide-receivers coach. Whether Morton or Webb ultimately controls play-calling duties on game days remains to be determined.

stories.headline: Broncos offseason depth chart: Key needs at running back, receiver entering NFL Combine and free agency
stories.summary:
With the NFL Scouting Combine arriving and free agency on the horizon, the Denver Broncos enter the 2026 offseason in a strong position after coming within four points of the Super Bowl. The team returns head coach Sean Payton, general manager George Paton, defensive coordinator Vance Joseph and quarterback Bo Nix heading into his third season, along with nearly a full complement of draft picks and significant salary cap space.

Running back is the position of greatest need after the room struggled without J.K. Dobbins in the second half of last season. RJ Harvey is the only back under contract without some form of free agency ahead, and the Broncos are expected to explore veteran options, with names such as Breece Hall, Travis Etienne and Kenneth Walker potentially available depending on what the free agent market yields.

At receiver, Denver returns Courtland Sutton, Marvin Mims Jr., Troy Franklin and Pat Bryant, giving the team a core of young pass catchers to build around. The team also faces a new offensive staff configuration, with Davis Webb now serving as coordinator and Logan Kilgore as quarterbacks coach following the departure of Joe Lombardi.

stories.headline: Avalanche name New Mexico Goatheads as new ECHL affiliate starting in 2026-27 season
stories.summary:
The Colorado Avalanche have secured a new ECHL affiliate, partnering with the New Mexico Goatheads beginning in the 2026-27 season. The announcement follows the relocation of the Utah Grizzlies to New Jersey, ending an eight-year affiliation between the Grizzlies and the Avalanche organization.

The New Mexico Goatheads are an expansion franchise entering the ECHL, making this partnership the first in the team's history. The Avalanche have long prioritized strong affiliate relationships as a cornerstone of player development, a philosophy that became a focal point under former president of hockey operations Joe Sakic.

Colorado's primary AHL affiliate remains the Colorado Eagles, who elevated from the ECHL to the AHL in 2018-19. The Utah Grizzlies joined the ECHL as an affiliate of the Eagles that same year, making the Grizzlies and Avalanche partners for the duration of that run.

stories.headline: Colorado Gov. Polis disinvited then reinstated to White House governors breakfast after last-minute reversal
stories.summary:
Colorado Gov. Jared Polis was among two governors initially disinvited from a White House breakfast intended to include all 50 state executives before the Trump administration reversed course and reinstated both men shortly before the event began. Maryland Gov. Wes Moore was the other governor caught up in the dispute, which drew national attention and threatened to prompt a broader boycott by fellow governors.

The National Governors Association, which typically co-hosts the event, withdrew from the breakfast after learning of the disinvitations. Political pressure mounted as other governors indicated they might decline to attend in solidarity, a dynamic that analysts said ultimately forced the White House to reverse its position.

Moore attended the event but said he would not engage in what he characterized as political theater. Trump asked the press to leave before the governors' discussion began. Political analysts said the episode raised the national profiles of both Moore and Polis while creating a public relations setback for the White House.

stories.headline: DPS board extends Kaiser Permanente health contract one year, orders new bidding process amid allegations of interference
stories.summary:
The Denver Public Schools board voted 6-1 Thursday to extend its existing health insurance contract with Kaiser Permanente for one year and reissue a Request for Proposals, rejecting a staff recommendation to award a three-year contract to Motiv Health and UnitedHealthcare. Superintendent Alex Marrero declined to support his own staff's recommendation, citing what he described as "a lot of meddling" in the procurement process.

Kaiser Permanente has held the DPS health insurance contract for more than 50 years but was outbid last year when the district solicited new proposals. MotivHealth earned the highest evaluation score of 77.7 out of 100, while Kaiser ranked last among four vendors at 69.3. After losing the bid, Kaiser submitted an additional proposal after the RFP process had closed and reached out directly to district staff, which a district spokesperson said amounted to an attempt to "improperly influence" the outcome.

DPS Chief of Talent Edwin Hudson was placed on administrative leave pending an investigation into allegations of inappropriate conduct during the bid process, though he was subsequently cleared of wrongdoing. An independent investigation also concluded that the district's original RFP process was valid and legal. The Denver Classroom Teachers Association, which represents roughly 4,200 teachers, had urged the board to extend the Kaiser contract and conduct a more transparent rebidding process, warning that poorly predicted healthcare costs ultimately fall on employees.

stories.headline: Law firm WilmerHale expands Denver footprint to 35,000 square feet in downtown tower
stories.summary:
International law firm WilmerHale has expanded its downtown Denver office, leasing an additional half floor at 1225 17th St. at the end of last year and bringing its total footprint to 35,000 square feet. The build-out is expected to cost nearly $1.2 million, according to public records.

The firm opened its Denver office 12 years ago with two attorneys and now employs 40 lawyers in the city. Partner-in-charge Mindy Sooter said the firm had been operating at capacity on its existing floor and described the office as "overflowing," citing consistent growth over the years and a four-day-a-week in-office requirement that many employees exceed.

WilmerHale practices at the intersection of government, technology and business and operates a dozen offices globally. The firm chose to remain in its current building, in part because the owner, Dallas-based Lone Star Funds, has plans to renovate the ground floor, outdoor plaza and third floor. Lone Star acquired the building last year for $132 million.

stories.headline: Colorado Army National Guard conducts patient-transport helicopter training at Denver Health
stories.summary:
Colorado Army National Guard crews conducted helicopter landing exercises at Denver Health on Friday afternoon, practicing procedures that would be used if Guard aircraft were needed to transport patients in an emergency. The training took place between 12:45 and 1:45 p.m. at the hospital's Pavilion A, located at 777 Bannock St. in central Denver.

A UH-72 Lakota helicopter performed controlled landings during the exercise. Denver Health officials said the training was designed to enhance coordination and readiness between the hospital and the National Guard, and noted that patient care would not be affected despite increased noise and helicopter activity in the area.

Residents in central Denver were advised to expect elevated helicopter activity during the training window. The exercise reflects ongoing efforts by military and civilian medical institutions to maintain interoperability for potential mass casualty or emergency transport scenarios.

stories.headline: Colorado Springs police log 303 arrests in one week, including double stabbing, domestic violence and vehicle in lake
stories.summary:
El Paso County law enforcement recorded 303 arrests during the week of Feb. 15-21, with the Colorado Springs Police Department posting 10 blotter entries covering incidents ranging from a double stabbing to a vehicle submerged in Prospect Lake. The summary was compiled from the department's public database of incidents approved for release.

On Feb. 15, officers responded to a double stabbing in southeast Colorado Springs in which two men were seriously injured following an argument; no arrests had been announced as of the report. The same day, Angel Quintana was arrested on felony domestic violence charges after threatening two women with a baseball bat and barricading himself inside a residence while refusing to comply with police commands.

On Thursday, officers and firefighters responded to Prospect Lake in Memorial Park after a vehicle crashed into the water and became fully submerged. The driver was rescued and taken to a hospital in critical condition. Two officers and two firefighters were treated for hypothermia and other injuries sustained during the rescue. In a separate incident earlier in the week, Amy Scheppach, 51, barricaded herself inside her home, claimed to have a firearm and threatened law enforcement, triggering a multi-hour shelter-in-place order on Hans Brinker Street.

stories.headline: Denver International Airport swaps land parcels with Commerce City to improve Second Creek Campus development prospects
stories.summary:
Denver International Airport has completed a land swap with Commerce City, exchanging 23.3 acres for 16.5 acres in a deal intended to improve development potential at the airport's Second Creek Campus. The transaction reflects ongoing efforts by DIA to optimize its landholdings for commercial and industrial development adjacent to the airport.

The Second Creek Campus has been identified as a key area for future airport-adjacent development, and officials indicated the new parcel configuration would make the site more attractive to prospective developers. Specific terms beyond the acreage exchange were not detailed in available reporting.

DIA has pursued a series of real estate and development initiatives in recent years as part of broader efforts to generate non-aviation revenue and attract businesses to the airport's surrounding area.

stories.headline: Denver housing affordability gap widens as average home price exceeds six times median family income
stories.summary:
The average Denver-area family must now spend more than six times its annual income to purchase a typical home in the metro area, more than double the commonly accepted affordability threshold of three times annual income, according to data highlighted in a Denver Business Journal report. The widening affordability gap is being cited by analysts as a significant drag on Colorado's ability to attract businesses and workers.

Economists and business analysts note that weak demographics, a stagnant or declining labor force, and elevated housing prices collectively reduce the state's competitiveness as a destination for corporate relocations and expansions. The report warns that sky-high home prices are compounding workforce challenges facing Colorado employers.

The findings arrive alongside separate data showing Denver ranks second-highest in the nation for construction costs, factors that together create a compounding pressure on housing supply and affordability. Policymakers and industry observers have pointed to these twin constraints as central challenges for the region's long-term economic health.

stories.headline: NFL Combine preview: Denver Broncos to evaluate draft prospects at Indianapolis with roster needs in focus
stories.summary:
The Denver Broncos are heading to the 2026 NFL Scouting Combine in Indianapolis with an eye toward targeted improvements rather than wholesale rebuilding after last season's AFC Championship Game appearance. Team personnel will evaluate prospects at the Indianapolis Convention Center and Lucas Oil Stadium through medical reviews, interviews, position drills and athletic testing.

With a nearly full complement of draft picks and significant salary cap space, the Broncos enter the combine with flexibility to address needs through both the draft and free agency. Running back and select defensive positions are expected to be priority areas of evaluation, given the depth losses sustained last season.

Coach Sean Payton and general manager George Paton will oversee the Broncos' combine work as the franchise looks to maintain the momentum of a deep playoff run while filling gaps in a roster built around quarterback Bo Nix heading into his third NFL season.

comic.teaser: "“Well, according to the dictionary, I’m just a large, flightless bird from Ea..." + 3 more
